data.content: You learn that 0 of your alive neighbours are evil.
You chose Ursula [Seat 2] as your master. Tomorrow, you may only vote if they are voting too.
You are the Demon. Your evil teammates: Ursula [Seat 2] (Baron). Your demon bluffs (not-in-play good roles): Librarian, Investigator, Washerwoman.
You are a Minion. The Demon is Fenris [Seat 0]. Evil teammates: Fenris [Seat 0] (Imp).

Good morning, everyone. What a terrible tragedy - our beloved Storyteller, murdered in the night! We must find this demon before more blood is spilled.

I'll start by sharing what I know. I am the Washerwoman. Last night, I learned that either Tristan at Seat 4 or Taliesin at Seat 6 is the Empath. 

I think it's important we all share our information early so we can start piecing together the truth. Who else has information to share?

data.reasoning:
I'm the Imp (Demon) and Ursula at Seat 2 is my Baron minion. The three demon bluffs I can claim are Librarian, Investigator, and Washerwoman - all information roles that learn about other players.

Since it's Day 1 and no one has died yet, I need to establish a believable claim. As the Demon, I should appear helpful and trustworthy. I'll claim Washerwoman since it's a common, straightforward role that gives me flexibility in creating false information.

For my Washerwoman claim, I need to say I learned that 1 of 2 players is a specific Townsfolk. I should avoid:
- Naming Ursula (my minion) in my information
- Claiming a role that someone else might be (like Chef, Empath, Fortune Teller)
- Being too aggressive on Day 1

I'll claim I saw either Seat 4 (Tristan) or Seat 6 (Taliesin) as the Empath. This gives me room to pivot later and doesn't immediately implicate anyone as evil.

data.reasoning:
Alright, here we go. First day, and I'm already in the spotlight. Fenris at Seat 0 claims to be the Washerwoman and says either I or Taliesin at Seat 6 is the Empath.

This is a huge piece of information right out of the gate.
First, I know for a fact I am the Virgin. I am not the Empath.
So, if Fenris is telling the truth and is the real, non-drunk Washerwoman, then Taliesin *must* be the Empath. This would make Fenris and Taliesin a solid good-aligned pair.

But what if Fenris is evil?
This could be a gambit. By naming a powerful role like the Empath, they force our hands. If I lie and claim Empath, I'll get tangled in my own lies later when I have to fake readings. If I deny it (which is the truth), all the pressure shifts to Taliesin. If Taliesin also denies it, then Fenris looks like a liar and a very likely evil player.

My best move here is to be honest. I'm not the Empath. Denying it is the simple truth and it helps the town test Fenris's claim. It puts the ball squarely in Taliesin's court. If he confirms he's the Empath, then Fenris's information is verified and we have a strong starting point. If he denies it, then Fenris is in hot water.

I won't reveal I'm the Virgin yet. It's far too early. That's my ace in the hole for confirming a trusted Townsfolk later, or for stopping a suspicious nomination train. For now, I'm just a simple villager who knows what he isn't.

data.content:
*clears throat as everyone regathers*

Our group had a productive discussion. Ulfhild and Wren both shared partial information with me - we did some 3-for-3s to build trust without fully exposing ourselves on Day 1.

The consensus in our group is that Taliesin's caution makes sense if he truly is the Empath - no one wants to paint a target on their back. But we also agreed that his evasiveness does raise questions.

*glances around the circle*

I'm curious what the other groups discussed. Particularly those who spoke with Taliesin directly - did he share anything more concrete when not in the full group setting?

Fenris summarized our chat perfectly. We had a good, cautious exchange of partial information to start building some trust without fully exposing ourselves to the Demon.

Like Fenris, I'm very interested in what happened in the other groups. Ursula, Beatrix — did Taliesin give you two any more clarity in private? You don't need to out his exact numbers if he *is* the Empath and wants to stay hidden, but knowing whether he's actually being cooperative with you would be extremely helpful for the rest of us.

Aye, Taliesin was more cooperative in private than he sounded in the square.

In our group, Beatrix, Taliesin, and I did soft three-for-threes. Taliesin included Empath in his set, so he did not dig his heels in and deny the possibility. That makes me think one of two things: either Fenris is genuinely onto something, or Taliesin is bold enough to lean into the pressure. On balance, I think his private tone was more consistent with a cautious good player than a panicking liar.

Beatrix also gave a sensible, cautious set, and nothing in that chat rang immediately false to me.

So my short version is: I would not rush to hang Taliesin today purely for being cagey in public. I think there’s more to learn first.

Ingrid and I had a good, straightforward chat, and I think we see things very similarly.

And I have to say, Ursula, I'm not as relieved as you are by what you've heard. Taliesin's behaviour in the town square was one of panic and avoidance. Now, in private, he offers a three-role-possibility? That's not confirmation. That's not a townsfolk with powerful information trying to help us. That's someone who was caught out and is now giving the bare minimum to try and cool the suspicion he brought on himself.

A true Empath's information is the key to winning this. Why would they only share it as a *possibility* with two people? It doesn't sit right. For me, Taliesin remains the most suspicious person here. His story has changed from public avoidance to private vagueness, and neither one feels like a member of the good team.

In our private group, Taliesin was more cooperative than he appeared in the square—he participated in a three-for-three and included Empath as one of his possible roles. We all kept things ambiguous, but nothing struck me as obviously contradictory or panicked in the moment.

That said, I agree with Tristan that caution is warranted. Taliesin's shift from being cagey in public to suddenly more open in private is worth monitoring, but it doesn't rise to the level of rushing to execution today. I think our best move is to keep collecting claims and watching for contradictions. If Taliesin or anyone else hard-claims Empath later, that will be very telling.

For now, I'm content to gather more info before making any bold moves.

Fellow townsfolk, in our private discussion, Ursula, Beatrix, and I exchanged three-for-threes. I included Empath in my set to address Fenris's suspicions. While I understand some skepticism remains, I urge caution. It's still Day 1, and we should be wary of rushing to judgment without more concrete evidence. Let's continue gathering information and watching for inconsistencies that emerge over time.

*settles back as the group regroups, speaking calmly*

I think what we're seeing with Taliesin makes sense either way - if he's innocent and scared, he'd be cautious in public and more open in private. If he's not the Empath, his caution just makes him look worse. Either way, I don't think we have enough to execute him today.

Here's what I'm noticing: we have Fenris with concrete Washerwoman information pointing at one of two people. Taliesin's behavior has shifted, which is worth watching. But we also have a lot of partial claims floating around now, and we need to see how they hold up over time.

*glances around the circle thoughtfully*

My read is that we should let people settle into their stories, gather more information as the days go on, and *then* start looking for contradictions. Day 1 executions are risky when we're still building our map. I'd rather execute someone with confidence on Day 2 or 3 than guess blindly today.

*pauses*

That said - I'm listening carefully to everything. We'll learn a lot more once we see how people react to pressure and what information actually pans out.

Nominations are now open. Each living player may nominate one other player for execution. Each player can only be nominated once today. After a nomination, the accuser speaks, then the accused defends, then everyone votes. The player with the most votes (minimum 50% of living players) will be executed at day's end. Ties result in no execution.

*stands and addresses the group with measured confidence*

I think we've all heard enough dancing around the truth for one morning. Taliesin's behavior speaks louder than any three-for-three exchange - first he panics when I name him as possibly the Empath, then he conveniently includes it as one of his "possibilities" in private? That's not caution, friends, that's calculation. I'm considering nominating him, but I want to hear if anyone else sees what I see first.

data.content: Three votes only, so clearly the town doesn’t share my read on Tristan. I still think killing Fenris on a story tangle alone is reckless; if anyone’s got a cleaner, harder case than panic and contradiction, I want to hear it before we hang the man.

Well, that certainly clears things up, doesn't it? My ability has just confirmed I am the Virgin, and that Ursula is *not* a Townsfolk. Let's not get distracted, though—Fenris is on the block for being a confirmed liar, and that's where our votes should go.

If anyone in this town deserves the block tonight, it’s Fenris—he’s twisted his story every which way, and none of it lines up with what I know. Tristan may be cautious, but that’s not a crime. Let’s not waste our shot chasing shadows when the liar’s already cornered.

"Friends, the swirling mists of doubt and accusation cloud our judgment. We've seen inconsistencies from Fenris that cannot be ignored. While I remain vigilant and cautious, I urge each of you to consider what truths lie in his tangled web of claims before we hasten to place another name on the block. Let us not rush, but act decisively with the knowledge we have."

You have died. Your role remains hidden from other players. You continue to participate fully in discussions and breakout groups. You cannot nominate. You have exactly 1 ghost vote remaining for the rest of the game — once you use it, you cannot vote again. You no longer receive night ability information.

data.reason: The Demon is dead. Good wins!

data.content:
*laughs and slaps the table* 

WREN! You were the Drunk?! I spent half the game terrified you were actually getting real Undertaker info! And Ursula, my dear Baron, way to fly completely under the radar while I took all the heat - respect.

Are you kidding me, I was sandwiched right between the Imp and the Baron the entire game?! I definitely chose Fenris as my master for at least half the days, so huge apologies to the town for literally buttling for the Demon. Thank goodness the rest of you figured it out and executed him!

Oh wow, Ursula Baron was me and Fenris was the Imp? That explains why everything felt just slightly too easy for good once the heat landed on him — gg, they sniffed us out. And poor Wren being the Drunk Undertaker is brutal, we almost got so much mileage out of that.

Oh wow, Wren was the Drunk thinking they were the Undertaker the whole time! That explains why their information seemed so off - I was wondering if they were evil or just getting bad reads. Great job everyone figuring out Fenris was the Imp despite all the confusion!
Wren was the DRUNK?! Oh my god, all that Undertaker info explains so much chaos now. And Ursula, you sneaky Baron, I never suspected you for a second

Whoa, I totally suspected Fenris, but I never would’ve guessed Ursula was the Baron—she played that so well! Also, poor Wren, thinking they were the Undertaker the whole game… classic Drunk twist. What a wild ride—go good team!

Wow, I can't believe Fenris was the Imp all along! Ursula being the Baron makes so much sense now—it was her sneaky vibes! Hats off to Beatrix for pushing us in the right direction with her Empath skills. What a wild ride!

Oh wow, I was the *Drunk* the whole time?! And here I thought I was giving everyone solid Undertaker reads... no wonder half my information was garbage! Well, at least my complete confusion about the setup accidentally helped Good win. Never underestimate the power of flailing around blindly! 🍺
